agent_output:
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "New 'Express Delivery' product offering faster courier delivery with a time-window commitment and premium fee. Spans ordering eligibility, driver assignment, real-time tracking, SLA enforcement, and refund processing. Raw notes from five stakeholders contain at least one hard contradiction (guarantee vs best-effort) that must be resolved before implementation.",
    "bounded_context": "Express Delivery Fulfillment — covers order eligibility, express dispatch, SLA tracking, driver assignment/replacement, and express-specific refund policy. Touches but does not own: Warehouse Inventory, Driver Management, Billing/Payments, Customer Communication.",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Express Delivery", "definition": "A premium delivery option with a 2-hour delivery window commitment and a $12.99 surcharge, available to customers within 10km of a warehouse." },
      { "term": "Express Fee", "definition": "The $12.99 surcharge applied on top of the base delivery fee when a customer selects Express Delivery." },
      { "term": "Base Delivery Fee", "definition": "The standard delivery charge, which is never refunded under the express SLA policy." },
      { "term": "Delivery Window", "definition": "The 2-hour period beginning at order placement within which the express delivery should be completed." },
      { "term": "Pickup SLA", "definition": "The 15-minute maximum time from order placement to physical pickup of the package from the warehouse." },
      { "term": "Express-Eligible Driver", "definition": "A driver with a rating of at least 4.0 stars who is qualified to fulfill express orders." },
      { "term": "Missed Window", "definition": "The condition where an express delivery is not completed within the 2-hour delivery window, triggering potential express fee refund." },
      { "term": "Driver Replacement Window", "definition": "The 10-minute period after a driver cancels mid-delivery within which a replacement driver must be assigned before an automatic refund is triggered." },
      { "term": "Goodwill Refund", "definition": "A refund of the express fee issued when the delivery window is missed — framed as a goodwill gesture, not a contractual obligation (per Legal)." },
      { "term": "Wrong Address Exclusion", "definition": "A refund exclusion where no express fee refund is issued if the customer provided an incorrect delivery address." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "Express Delivery is only available to customers whose delivery address is within 10km of a warehouse.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Physical constraint — the 2-hour window is impossible to meet beyond this radius. Offering express outside this range produces undeliverable commitments.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a delivery address >10km from the nearest warehouse, the Express Delivery option must not be selectable."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "The express fee is $12.99, applied as a surcharge on top of the base delivery fee.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Pricing is a business policy that can be adjusted by product/finance leadership.",
      "testable_assertion": "When a customer selects Express Delivery, the order total must include a $12.99 express fee line item separate from the base delivery fee."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "Express orders must be picked up from the warehouse within 15 minutes of order placement.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Operational constraint — exceeding 15 minutes on pickup makes the 2-hour end-to-end window unachievable. This is a physical/time constraint, not a policy choice.",
      "testable_assertion": "If an express order has not been picked up within 15 minutes of placement, the system must escalate (exact escalation path TBD — see escalations)."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "Only drivers with a rating of 4.0 stars or above may be assigned to express orders.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Rating threshold is a quality policy that operations leadership could adjust (e.g., to 3.5 or 4.5) based on driver supply.",
      "testable_assertion": "The dispatch system must never assign a driver with a rating below 4.0 to an express order."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "When an express delivery misses the 2-hour window, the express fee ($12.99) is refunded but the base delivery fee is not.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Refund policy is a business decision that could be changed by finance/product leadership.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a completed express delivery where delivery_time > order_placement_time + 2 hours, a refund of exactly the express fee amount must be initiated."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "No express fee refund is issued if the customer provided an incorrect delivery address, even if the delivery window was missed.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "This is a refund exclusion policy, adjustable by business decision.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a missed express window where the delivery address was flagged as incorrect by the driver, no express fee refund must be issued."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-7",
      "rule": "Express fee refunds are processed within 3 business days.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Processing timeline is an operational SLA, not a system integrity constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a triggered express fee refund, the refund must reach 'processed' status within 3 business days of the missed window event."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-8",
      "rule": "If an express driver cancels mid-delivery, a replacement express-eligible driver must be assigned within 10 minutes.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The 10-minute replacement window is an operational target, not a physical constraint. It could be adjusted.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a driver cancellation on an active express delivery, a replacement driver must be assigned within 10 minutes or an automatic refund is triggered."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-9",
      "rule": "If no replacement driver is assigned within 10 minutes of a mid-delivery cancellation, the express fee is automatically refunded.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Automatic refund trigger is a customer experience policy.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a driver cancellation where 10 minutes elapse with no replacement assigned, the system must auto-initiate an express fee refund without manual intervention."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-10",
      "rule": "Express delivery customers must have access to real-time driver tracking.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Tracking is a product feature, not a system integrity constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given an active express delivery, the customer must be able to view the driver's current location updated at least every 30 seconds (update frequency TBD — see escalations)."
    }
  ],

  "acceptance_criteria": [],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "Customer is exactly 10km from the warehouse", "expected_behavior": "Must define whether 10km is inclusive or exclusive. Recommendation: inclusive (<=10km is eligible)." },
    { "scenario": "Express order placed but no express-eligible driver is available within 15 minutes", "expected_behavior": "Undefined in notes. Must define: reject order upfront? Accept and escalate? Auto-refund? This is a critical gap." },
    { "scenario": "Driver cancels at minute 115 of a 120-minute window", "expected_behavior": "Replacement within 10 minutes is impossible before the 2-hour window expires. Both BR-8 and BR-5 trigger. Should only one refund be issued (not double)." },
    { "scenario": "Customer changes address after order placement to one >10km away", "expected_behavior": "Undefined. Does the express commitment still hold? Should the order be downgraded to standard?" },
    { "scenario": "Driver picks up at minute 14 but gets stuck in traffic — delivery at 2h05m", "expected_behavior": "Missed window refund applies per BR-5. But who bears accountability — driver or system? Affects driver rating/compensation." },
    { "scenario": "Customer provides correct address but is unreachable at delivery time", "expected_behavior": "Does customer unavailability count as 'wrong address exclusion'? Likely not — needs its own rule." },
    { "scenario": "Multiple driver cancellations on the same express order", "expected_behavior": "First cancellation triggers 10-minute replacement window. If second driver also cancels, does the 10-minute clock restart? At what point is the order force-refunded?" },
    { "scenario": "Warehouse is out of stock after express order is placed", "expected_behavior": "Completely unaddressed. Is this a full refund (express + base) or just express fee?" },
    { "scenario": "Refund triggered automatically (BR-9) but address was wrong (BR-6)", "expected_behavior": "Conflict: auto-refund from driver replacement failure vs wrong-address exclusion. Which rule wins?" },
    { "scenario": "Express order placed at 11:55 PM — 3 business day refund window spans a weekend", "expected_behavior": "Must clarify 'business days' calculation and timezone handling." }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "Express fee ($12.99) is a new revenue stream but carries refund liability. Aggressive SLA (2 hours, 15-min pickup, 10-min replacement) will produce a non-trivial refund rate initially. The 'wrong address exclusion' limits fraud/abuse exposure. Refund processing within 3 business days has cash flow implications — automated refunds from BR-9 may bypass normal finance review.",
    "operational": "Constraining express to 4-star+ drivers reduces the eligible driver pool significantly. The 15-minute pickup SLA requires warehouse readiness and driver proximity — may require dedicated express driver pools or geo-fencing. Real-time tracking increases infrastructure load. The 10-minute replacement window demands an always-available backup dispatch mechanism.",
    "user": "Strong value proposition: fast delivery with fee-back guarantee builds trust. Real-time tracking increases engagement and reduces 'where is my order' support tickets. However, the legal 'best effort' language vs prominent '2-hour guarantee' display creates a trust gap if customers read the fine print.",
    "risk": "The guarantee vs best-effort contradiction is the highest risk item — marketing a 'guarantee' that Legal says isn't one exposes the company to regulatory scrutiny, consumer protection complaints, and brand damage if refund volumes are high and Legal blocks payouts. Driver supply constraints (4-star+, 15-min pickup) may cause frequent SLA breaches in early rollout.",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "Customers", "impact": "New premium delivery option with time commitment and tracking. Risk of confusion between 'guarantee' marketing and 'best effort' legal reality." },
      { "group": "Drivers", "impact": "New express tier with stricter performance requirements (4-star+, 15-min pickup). May need incentive structure for express assignments." },
      { "group": "Warehouse Operations", "impact": "Must support 15-minute pickup SLA — requires prioritization queues and express-ready packaging workflows." },
      { "group": "Finance/Billing", "impact": "New fee line item, new refund type, automated refund triggers that bypass manual approval, 3-business-day processing commitment." },
      { "group": "Customer Support", "impact": "Must handle express-specific inquiries, wrong-address disputes, and refund status questions. Need clear escalation paths." },
      { "group": "Legal/Compliance", "impact": "Must approve all customer-facing language. 'Guarantee' wording is explicitly rejected by Legal — any implementation using that word is non-compliant." }
    ]
  },

  "confidence": {
    "level": 35,
    "reasoning": "Hard contradiction between PM ('guaranteed') and Legal ('best effort, not a guarantee') makes the core product promise undefined. Until this is resolved, we cannot write acceptance criteria because we don't know what we're promising the customer. Additionally, multiple critical operational gaps (no-driver-available scenario, stock-outs, escalation paths for missed pickup SLA) mean the domain model is incomplete. The individual rules from each stakeholder are internally coherent, but they have not been reconciled into a single consistent policy.",
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Express fee amount ($12.99) and separation from base delivery fee",
      "10km eligibility radius as a hard constraint",
      "4-star driver rating threshold for express eligibility",
      "Wrong address exclusion from refund policy",
      "Real-time tracking as a required feature"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Whether the 2-hour window is a guarantee or best-effort SLA — this changes everything about refund obligations and customer-facing language",
      "What happens when no express-eligible driver is available at order time",
      "Escalation path when 15-minute pickup SLA is breached",
      "How automatic refund (BR-9) interacts with wrong-address exclusion (BR-6)",
      "Whether the delivery window clock starts at order placement or pickup"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "The 2-hour delivery window starts at order placement time (not pickup time) — PM said 'within 2 hours' without specifying start point",
      "Driver rating of 4.0 is on a 5-star scale",
      "10km distance is calculated as straight-line (geodesic), not driving distance",
      "Only one express fee refund is issued per order regardless of how many refund triggers fire",
      "Real-time tracking means GPS-based location updates visible to the customer in-app"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "contradiction",
      "description": "Product Manager states the 2-hour window is a 'guarantee' and Customer Success wants it 'shown prominently.' Legal explicitly states it is NOT a guarantee — it is a 'best effort SLA' and the refund is a 'goodwill gesture, not legally enforceable.' This is a fundamental contradiction about the core product promise. If we build it as a guarantee with automatic refunds, Legal may block launch. If we build it as best-effort, the product value proposition weakens and refund logic becomes discretionary rather than automatic.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Product Management", "Legal", "Customer Success", "Customers", "Finance"],
      "options": [
        "A: Build as a guarantee with automatic refunds — Legal must sign off on updated terms of service",
        "B: Build as best-effort with discretionary goodwill refunds — Product/CS must accept weaker customer promise",
        "C: Hybrid — market as 'Express Promise' (not 'guarantee'), auto-refund as default but with exclusions, Legal approves specific language"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C — 'Express Promise' avoids the legal term 'guarantee' while still enabling automatic refunds with clear exclusions. Requires a joint session between PM, Legal, and CS to agree on customer-facing language and refund trigger rules."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "No stakeholder addressed what happens when an express order is placed but no express-eligible driver (4-star+) is available within the 15-minute pickup window. This is a high-frequency scenario, especially at launch with a limited express driver pool.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Operations", "Customers", "Customer Support"],
      "options": [
        "A: Reject the express option at order time if no driver is estimated available (pre-check)",
        "B: Accept the order and escalate if pickup SLA is missed",
        "C: Accept and auto-downgrade to standard delivery with express fee refund"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A with fallback to C — check driver availability before offering express. If availability changes between offer and placement, auto-downgrade with refund and notify customer."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "The delivery window is stated as '2 hours' but the start point is never defined. Does the clock start at order placement, payment confirmation, or warehouse pickup? This changes the operational feasibility — if it starts at placement, the 15-minute pickup SLA consumes 12.5% of the window.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Operations", "Product Management", "Customers"],
      "options": [
        "A: Clock starts at order placement (strictest, matches customer expectation)",
        "B: Clock starts at pickup (gives operations more flexibility but customer sees longer total time)"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A — from the customer's perspective, the 2 hours starts when they place the order. Any other definition will feel dishonest. The 15-minute pickup SLA already accounts for this."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "Finance says refunds process within '3 business days,' but Customer Success says replacement failure triggers an 'automatic refund.' These may not conflict (auto-trigger vs processing time), but the customer expectation gap is significant. Does 'automatic' mean instant credit or instant initiation with 3-day processing?",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Finance", "Customer Success", "Customers"],
      "options": [
        "A: Auto-trigger means instant refund to payment method (Finance must support this)",
        "B: Auto-trigger means refund is initiated immediately but processed within normal 3-business-day cycle",
        "C: Auto-trigger issues instant account credit; payment method refund follows in 3 days"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C — instant account credit gives the customer immediate satisfaction while Finance retains the 3-day processing window for the actual payment reversal."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "No definition of how 'wrong address' is determined or disputed. Who decides the address is wrong — the driver, the system, or the customer? What evidence is required? This exclusion (BR-6) blocks refunds, so it needs a clear, auditable determination process to avoid customer disputes.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customer Support", "Drivers", "Customers", "Legal"],
      "options": [
        "A: Driver flags wrong address with photo evidence, customer support reviews",
        "B: System detects mismatch between GPS delivery location and stated address",
        "C: Both — automated detection with driver confirmation and customer dispute path"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C — automated detection as primary signal, driver confirmation as supporting evidence, with a customer dispute workflow. Legal should review the dispute resolution process."
    }
  ],

  "rejection_reasons": [
    { "violation": "Cannot proceed to acceptance criteria while the guarantee vs best-effort contradiction is unresolved", "business_rule_broken": "The core product promise (BR-5, BR-9 refund triggers) depends on whether this is a contractual obligation or discretionary goodwill — these produce fundamentally different system behaviors." },
    { "violation": "No handling defined for the no-driver-available scenario", "business_rule_broken": "BR-3 (15-minute pickup) and BR-4 (4-star+ drivers) create a supply constraint with no defined fallback, which will cause order failures in production." }
  ]
}
